title: chaetacalia stylotricha
intro: Chaetacalia stylotricha, also known as Himalayan blue coleus, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and reducing fever. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids and saponins, providing various health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, as well as supporting digestive and respiratory health. In traditional herbal preparations, Chaetacalia stylotricha is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
